entries.title: How hard is it to build orbital data centers, actually?
entries.summary: "The ISS radiators are expensive and heavy. We're focused on making them cheap and light."

entries.title: Sotheby's big T. rex auction raises concerns hype and wealth are upending science
entries.summary: Private buyers are increasingly outbidding museums for fossils.

entries.title: Microsoft’s Secure Boot has been broken for a decade and no one noticed until now
entries.summary: Old and forgotten "shims" Microsoft failed to revoke have made Secure Boot bypasses simple.

entries.title: Trump admin puts Americans in Congo on "do-not-board" list, barring return
entries.summary: Citizens must now spend 21 days in a third country before they are allowed to come home.

entries.title: Lawsuit claims Meta's layoff decisions were made by AI, not humans
entries.summary: Meta denies using AI to terminate workers with disabilities and medical problems.

entries.title: Probe into explosive diarrheal cases points to Taco Bell and bad lettuce
entries.summary: Federal officials have not confirmed a source yet—and there may be multiple sources.

entries.title: US military sent explosive drone boats into combat for the first time
entries.summary: US military’s drone boats struck an Iranian naval port as war heats up again.

entries.title: These painted e-tattoos could be the future of wearable biosensors
entries.summary: Conductive ink is painted directly onto the skin in colorful custom designs, drying into working electrodes.

entries.title: Google revamps image search for its 25th anniversary with more images and more AI
entries.summary: The new Google image search will use your "unique interests" to create an always-updated gallery.

entries.title: New York bans data center construction for a year, rattling AI industry
entries.summary: New York’s data center moratorium may become the blueprint for anti-AI movement.
